strategy: He opens with a -65% Tower Shield and re-shields after every hit, so bring Pierce or Shatter, or use Steal Ward to strip it safely (and keep the shield yourself).
Avoid multi-hit spells (Minotaur, Hydra) — each hit makes him add another shield.
Attack with Myth or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Storm resist without a prism.
In a group you can strip his shields by casting a single-target charm on a teammate (he Cleanses all his own wards) — but it also removes your traps on him, so time it before the hitter goes.
Stack blades, break the Tower the same round, and land one clean single hit.
cheatNotes: He starts with a -65% Tower Shield. Pierce or shatter through it, or Steal Ward it off (and keep the shield yourself).
Steal Ward and Pierce remove the Tower safely without provoking a replacement — use them over raw damage.
He re-casts the -65% Tower by interrupt after every attack on him, so break the shield the same round you commit your big hit.
Multi-hit spells (Hydra, Minotaur) make him stack a shield per hit — use a single-hit attack instead.
Casting a single-target charm on a teammate makes him Cleanse off all his own shields, traps, and prisms — handy to clear his Tower, but it wipes your traps on him too.
Vaporize dispels this Cleanse cheat if you want to stop him from stripping his own wards.
